Paths/Full Stack Development/Git & Collaboration

Git & Collaboration

Work effectively with teams using Git, GitHub, and open source workflows.

Git is the version control system that powers modern software collaboration.

Key Concepts

  • Repositories: A project folder tracked by Git.
  • Branches: Work on features in isolation, then merge into main.
  • Commits: Snapshots of your code at a point in time.
  • Pull Requests: Propose changes and get code reviews before merging.
  • Merge Conflicts: When two branches change the same lines — resolve manually.

Common Workflow

# Create a feature branch
git checkout -b feature/add-navbar

# Make changes and commit
git add src/components/Navbar.tsx
git commit -m "Add responsive navbar component"

# Push and create PR
git push origin feature/add-navbar
gh pr create --title "Add navbar" --body "Adds responsive navigation"
